back_translation: King Ahasuerus imposed tribute upon the land and upon the islands of the sea as well.

As for all his royal acts, full of his power and might, and the account of the greatness of Mordecai, whom the king had promoted — are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Media and Persia?

For Mordecai the Jew was viceroy, second to King Ahasuerus, great among the Jews, and favored by the multitude of his fellow kinsmen; he sought the welfare of his people and spoke words of peace to all their descendants.
